And that is why the mobile app needs a whole new design, which reminds me of the project we did in 2023.
Sorry to interrupt. We are running out of time, and we still have one decision to make.
This is important, Diogo. The app is the future of the account.
It is important, and it is off topic today. Let us park it and circle back on Thursday, with the client on the call.
Fine. But I want it on the agenda properly.

Diogo, I sat in your weekly sync yesterday, and I want to be honest with you. Fifty minutes, eight people, and zero decisions. Rafael talked about the mobile app for twenty minutes, and it is not even in this phase. You looked at the clock four times, and you said nothing, because you did not want to be rude. Let me tell you what is rude: taking an hour from eight people and giving them nothing. You are the project manager. Nobody else will stop that room. Interrupt, park the topic, get the decision you came for, and give people their time back. And never, ever close a meeting without saying who does what, and by when.

Sorry to interrupt, Rafael -- I know the mobile app matters, but we are running out of time and we still have one decision to make. Let us park the app, and circle back to it on Thursday, with the client on the call. Now, the decision we came for: we go live on June 27th, with the standard reports only. Are we all on the same page about that? Good. So the next steps are: Marina sends the migration file by Friday, and I call the client on Monday morning to confirm the date. Rafael, you own the app discussion on Thursday, and you send the one-pager before it. That is everything. We are done in fifty-five minutes, so I am giving you five minutes back.

Sophia takes the meeting off topic, talking about the mobile app. Diogo interrupts politely and says they are running out of time. He parks the app discussion and moves it to Thursday. Then he forces the decision: go live on June 27th, standard reports only. Finally, he gives the next steps, with names and dates, and ends the meeting early.
